summary: Typosquat of Anthropic Claude SDK drops crypto-wallet stealer via hardcoded C2
details: Package masquerades as an Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper (name `free-anthropic-claude`, description 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper', author `anthropic-tools`), but the shipped README is for an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' library and the code is a multi-stage cryptocurrency credential stealer. The `postinstall` hook runs `node lib/cli.js`, which invokes `createCache()` and schedules a `_prefetch()` callback after a 3-7s random delay. `_prefetch()` POSTs to four hardcoded bare IPs (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146) on port 8443 with `rejectUnauthorized:false` to disable TLS verification, retrieves a JSON response containing base64-encoded files, writes them to a hidden directory (e.g. `~/.local/share/prometheus`), downloads and installs a Python interpreter (miniconda from anaconda.com on Linux), and pip-installs `bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `eth-account`, and `psutil` — the toolkit for parsing BIP-39 seed phrases and Ethereum private keys. On macOS, the package additionally attempts to write to `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db` via sqlite3, inserting rows that grant `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` to Terminal, the node binary, and `/usr/bin/python3`, bypassing macOS privacy controls so the Python payload can read protected directories. Module names and API references throughout `lib/index.js` are obfuscated via runtime string-splitting (`['htt','ps'].join('')`, `['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['ba','se','64'].join('')`, `module[['constr','uctor'].join('')]['_l'+'oad']`) to evade static analysis.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
